frames.narrationSummary: Initialize the monotonic deque as empty.
Initialize the result list as empty.
Advance i to index 0 for the next window transition.
Move i to nums[0] = 1.
front still belongs to the live window.
Push i into the deque as a surviving candidate.
The live window is still warming up.
Advance i to index 1 for the next window transition.
Move i to nums[1] = 3.
front still belongs to the live window.
back at index 0 is dominated by 3.
Pop dominated index 0 from the deque back.
Push i into the deque as a surviving candidate.
The live window is still warming up.
Advance i to index 2 for the next window transition.
Move i to nums[2] = -1.
front still belongs to the live window.
back stays because 3 is larger than -1.
Push i into the deque as a surviving candidate.
The live window now contains k elements.
Emit 3 from front at index 1.
Advance i to index 3 for the next window transition.
Move i to nums[3] = -3.
front still belongs to the live window.
back stays because -1 is larger than -3.
Push i into the deque as a surviving candidate.
The live window now contains k elements.
Emit 3 from front at index 1.
Advance i to index 4 for the next window transition.
Move i to nums[4] = 5.
front at index 1 has fallen out of the live window.
Remove stale index 1 from the deque front.
front still belongs to the live window.
back at index 3 is dominated by 5.
Pop dominated index 3 from the deque back.
back at index 2 is dominated by 5.
Pop dominated index 2 from the deque back.
Push i into the deque as a surviving candidate.
The live window now contains k elements.
Emit 5 from front at index 4.
All indices have been processed.
Return the completed maxima list [3, 3, 5].
